title: Plan a beach getaway around a concert at Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom will undergo a $600 million redevelopment starting post-2027 summer season.
The 2026 concert lineup at Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom includes Melissa Etheridge, Clint Black, and David Lee Roth.
The redevelopment includes a 3,500-seat venue, boutique hotel, luxury condos, retail space, and a parking garage.

title: Bucket truck overturns into oncoming traffic; worker seriously injured
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A bucket truck overturned into oncoming traffic on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, injuring a worker seriously, police report.
The incident occurred around 9 a.m. and led to the closure of Memorial Drive between North Harvard Street and JFK Street.
The State Police and OSHA are investigating the crash, which has kept the road closed for several hours.

title: Healey congratulates WHOOP on plans to expand, add 600 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: WHOOP to add 600 jobs, focusing on Kenmore Square headquarters, expanding its presence in Boston.
Gov. Maura Healey praises WHOOP's expansion as a boost to Massachusetts' economy and talent pool.
WHOOP, founded in 2012, aims to enhance global market reach by combining talent and AI advancements.

title: Here’s the Eagles’ reported asking price for a Patriots A.J. Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Eagles' asking price for A.J. Brown is similar to a Quinnen Williams-type deal, including a first-round pick and second-round sweetener.
Patriots showed interest in trading for A.J. Brown after their Super Bowl loss and passing game struggles.
A.J. Brown recorded 78 catches for 1,003 yards and seven touchdowns with the Eagles in the 2025 season.

title: Here are the Mass. snow totals from Tuesday’s wintry mix
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Franklin County saw the highest snowfall with Northfield receiving 6.5 inches and Warwick reporting 6 inches.
Freezing rain followed the snow, reaching 0.13 inches in Sterling and 0.1 inches in Haverhill, complicating the commute.
Suffolk County, including Boston, experienced lighter snow with Fenway Park area receiving 1.6 inches of accumulation.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (March 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix offers a curated list of top movies, ranging from Netflix originals to classics, organized by genre.
Highlighted films include "The Lego Movie," "Misery," and "Zombieland," all currently streaming on Netflix.
"His Three Daughters" stars Natasha Lyonne, Elizabeth Olsen, and Carrie Coon navigating family dynamics in a Manhattan apartment.

title: After losing her brother to suicide, she’s running Boston to help others feel less alone
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jen Hoye, 53, from Taunton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her brother Teddy.
Teddy, a beloved DCR park ranger, died by suicide in 2017, deeply impacting Jen's life.
Hoye runs with Team Samaritans to support the 988 crisis lifeline and highlight suicide prevention.

title: Residents voice frustrations with snow removal during City Council hearing
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Residents with disabilities reported dangerous conditions due to inadequate snow removal during Boston City Council hearing.
Proposals discussed included purchasing snow melting machines and creating volunteer 'snow corps' for assisting vulnerable residents.
Officials faced challenges with snow removal coordination between city, MBTA, businesses, and limited staffing for pilot programs.

title: RFK Jr. blasts Dunkin’ as Healey replies, ‘come and take it’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Health Secretary RFK Jr. criticized Dunkin' for selling sugary drinks without proven safety data at an Austin rally.
Gov. Maura Healey responded to RFK Jr.'s comments by resharing a meme supporting Dunkin' on social media.
Kennedy's statements sparked social media reactions referencing New England's cultural ties to Dunkin' and historical events.

title: Stats in Southie is closing for now, but has plans to reopen ‘bigger and better’
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Stats Bar & Grille in South Boston is closing at the end of March, planning to reopen in 2027.
The new development on Dorchester Street includes 15 residential units and a two-story restaurant space.
Owners Andrew and Jim Statires aim for a late 2027 reopening, depending on construction progress.
